message:
refactor(palette): use layout tier system for responsive breakpoints

Align the command palette's responsive layout decisions with the
unified layout tier system from internal/tui/layout.

Previous behavior:
- Palette used its own hardcoded splitViewThreshold (90)
- Layout mode checks were duplicated and could drift from dashboard

New behavior:
- Import and use layout.TierForWidth for tier detection
- Split view enabled when tier >= TierSplit (120 columns)
- Ultra-wide proportions when tier == TierUltra OR layoutMode is
  LayoutUltraWide (for backwards compatibility)
- Wide display uses tier >= TierWide for generous proportions

Layout proportions remain similar but are now derived from the same
tier thresholds used by the dashboard, ensuring consistent behavior
across all TUI surfaces.

Additional cleanup:
- Removed unused splitViewThreshold constant
- Normalized style arithmetic expressions (width-2 instead of width - 2)
- Preview width calculation now uses subtraction from total width
  instead of fixed percentage to avoid overflow in edge cases

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
